name: Duck Stock- Master Chefs
recipeIngredient: 5 lb Duck, parts (backs, necks, carcasses, and giblets, no livers)
2 medium Carrots, peeled, trimmed, coarsely chopped
2 large Celery stalks, with leaves, trimmed, coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash duck parts well and place them in a large stockpot.
2. Add cold water to cover by about 2 inches and slowly bring to a boil, skimming all froth from the surface as it forms.
3. Lower heat and add all remaining ingredients except peppercorns. Simmer uncovered for 3 hours, adding water if necessary to cover ingredients and skim when needed.
4. Add peppercorns for last fifteen minutes of simmering.
5. Strain "soup" into a large bowl through a colander lined with double layer dampened cheesecloth. Gently press solids to extract liquid.
6. Discard solids and cool liquid to room temperature.
7. Refrigerate until chilled, lift off solid fat that forms on surface, discard fats.
8. Pour stock into containers for storage, label and date.
9. Stock keeps 3-4 days refrigerated and up to six months frozen.
